channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Marine Le Pen branding as 'victim of judicial harassment' would be 'political stretch'
item.description: Marine Le Pen is set to give a crucial interview on French primetime television on July 7 following a sentence handed down by a court of appeal after she was found guilty of systemising the embezzlement of EU funds within her party, the National Rally. Although she has said she would not run with an electronic tag on, she could well change her mind. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent looks at the implications of such a decision.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Decision time' for far right National Rally as ruling clears Le Pen's way for election run
item.description: Will Marine Le Pen leader be running in the 2027 presidential election? Or will she step back and let her popular 30-year-old protégé Jordan Bardella run instead? That is the question the party will have to answer following the appeal court's decision to bar Le Pen for office for 15 months, which could in theory allow her to run - although with an electronic tag, which she has already said would be a no-go. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman and Luke Shrago tell us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Le Pen now 'has a choice' to run in the election or not
item.description: A Paris appeal's court cleared the way July 7 for popular far-right leader Marine Le Pen to possibly run for the French presidency in 2027, but said she must wear an electronic bracelet. But she has said she would not in those conditions in the past. Now, the ball is in her court: she must decide whether she has to campaign with a monitoring bracelet or not. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: What has French far-right leader Marine Le Pen been found guilty of and sentenced to?
item.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has been found guilty of embezzling EU fund, with the appeal's court sentencing her to a 15-month ineligibility for office and a year with an electronic tag. Technically this would allow her to run for the presidential election, but she has said she would not run with an electronic tag. FRANCE 2''s Catherine Norris-Trent is at the appeal's court in Paris and tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France's Le Pen cleared to run in 2027,with ankle bracelet
item.description: A French appeal court on Tuesday upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for embezzling EU funds but shortened her ban on running for public office, in theory giving a path ​for the far-right ‌leader to run in the 2027 presidential election. The court sentenced Le Pen to ⁠a three-year jail term: two suspended and one with an electronic ankle tag. She ​is due to give a prime-time TV interview on TF1 at 8pm (1800 GMT), in which ‌she may make an announcement on her political future.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Who is Jordan Bardella, French far right's Marine Le Pen's protégé?
item.description: French far -right figurehead Marine Le Pen has been barred from public office for 15 months and sentenced to a year under electronic card after an appeal's course found her guilty of embezzling EU funds. Although this means she could run in the election, Le Pen always said she would not run for office with an electronic tag - meaning her protégé Jordan Bardella is likely to become the far right's presidential candidate. But who exactly is he?
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Le Pen sentenced to 15-month ban from public office in EU embezzlement case
item.description: A French ​appeals court on ​July 7 upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing EU funds ​but shortened ‌her ban ⁠on running for elected office. In theory, this potentially re-opens a path for the ⁠far-right leader to run in the 2027 presidential ​race. But Le Pen is also sentenced to wearing an electronic tag for a year. She had said herself she would not run if that were the case. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent tells us the latest from the court.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo out of his last World Cup as Spain beat Portugal
item.description: Mikel Merino broke the deadlock in injury time as Spain continue their perfect run with a 1-0 win. Portugal's exit means Cristiano Ronaldo played his final game at the World Cup, as he confirmed he's retiring from international football. Spain will face Belgium next after Rudi Garcia's side thrashed the United States 4-1 following the controversy caused by US President Donald Trump and FIFA. The football association reversed a refereeing decision ahead of the game after being asked to do so by the US leader.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: 🔴 Live: Marine Le Pen convicted on appeal, but may still stand in the 2027 presidential election
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 🔴 Follow live: French court upholds Le Pen's fraud conviction, threatening her 2027 presidential hopes
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Woman suspected in Monaco bombing found shot dead near Kyiv, prosecutors say
item.description: The body of the Ukrainian woman suspected of planting a parcel bomb in Monaco last week was found near Kyiv, the local newspaper Ukrainska Pravda reported Tuesday. Ukraine's prosecutor general's ‌office later confirmed the body was found and said they also detained an employee of ​Ukraine's ​military intelligence ​service who said he ​killed the woman together with another ⁠suspect.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Syria: Macron ‘safe’ after blasts near French president’s hotel
item.description: Two bomb explosions rocked Syria’s Damascus on Tuesday, close to the hotel where French President Emmanuel Macron was staying as he visited the country. The Élysée palace said Macron was safe, with his motorcade seen leaving the hotel before the explosions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: German Gen Z joins the Bundeswehr: The military becomes a plan B
item.description: Germany is rearming amid an economic recession with thousands of jobs slashed across industrial and service sectors. In these tough economic conditions, the Bundeswehr is now one of the country's most attractive employers for young people. Applications rose by 23 percent in January 2026 alone. Behind the figures lies a more complicated reality: for many young Germans, putting on a uniform is not a vocation – it's a fallback option. FRANCE 24’s Anne Mailliet reports, with Nick Holdsworth.
item.dc:creator: Anne MAILLIET

item.title: What to know about Macron's landmark visit to Syria
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Syria is historic: he is the first EU leader to visit the country since the rebels took over and ousted dictator al-Assad. Both Macron and al-Sharaa hope to build a path towards Syria's rehabilitation, but two unclaimed explosions in Damascus near Macron's hotel shows that there are still major security challenges to overcome. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more on what to know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At the World Cup, Belgium knock out US team tainted by red card controversy
item.description: Donald Trump intervention wasn’t enough. Belgium have beaten and knocked the United States out of the World Cup. A 4-1 victory for the Red Devils in a match where all eyes were on Florian Balogun, the US striker who had been shown a red card and was not due to play until FIFA lifted his suspension followin an appeal by Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'France stands by Syria's side': Macron 'most enthusiastic in Europe' to repair ties
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has told Syria that 'France stands by its side' at a press conference in Damascus during his landmark visit to Ahmed al-Sharaa. Both leaders are hoping to bring more investment into the war-torn country as it looks to rebuild, although security challenges still remain - and were highlighted by a twin explosion near Macron's hotel. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more about why Macron is so keen on normalising Europe's relationship with Syria.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Replay: French President Emmanuel Macron speaks after Damascus blasts
item.description: Rewatch Macron and al-Sharaa's press conference during the French president's landmark visit and after two bombs exploded in Damascus, near Macron's hotel.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Al-Sharaa's capacity to bring security to Syria and the region under scrutiny
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is visiting Syria's leader Ahmed al-Sharaa ahead of a Nato summit on July 7. Even though the two leaders are expected to sign several contracts crucial for Syria's reconstruction, security is the main concern - both inside the country, but also in the region. Western leaders are increasingly testing al-Sharaa's capacity to ovecomes those security challenges. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman and Clovis Casali tell us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Syria's rehabilitation 'suits the West' while Damascus hopes to attract investors
item.description: At least 18 people have been wounded after two bombs exploded in the centre of Damascus near the hotel where French President Emmanuel Macron is staying. This comes as Ahmed al-Sharaa is trying to convince the world that Syria is dealing with important security challenges, in a bid to attract foreign investment to rebuild the war-torn country. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Samsung Electronics profits surge 1,800% annually amid artificial intelligence spending boom
item.description: South Korean chipmaker Samsung Electronics posted record quarterly profits Tuesday thanks to the global AI infrastructure buildout coupled with a memory chip shortage. Also in this edition, oil futures rise as tensions reignite in the Hormuz Strait and Canada commits to its biggest-ever defence contract. Finally, Cuba experiences its third nationwide blackout of the year as its fuel reserves dwindle.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: Explosions trying to deter 'business as usual' strategy at the heart of Macron's visit
item.description: As French President Emmanuel Macron is holding a landmark visit to his Syrian counterpart, several bombs exploded near Damascus's Four Seasons Hotel, in the city centre, where Macron is said to be staying. But the two presidents seem undeterred as the visit signs a landmark moment for Syria's post-Assad: Macron is the first Western leader to be visiting Syria since al-Sharaa came to power. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman and Clovis Casali tell us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Syria's 'security challenges' main obstacle to attracting investors
item.description: Explosions rocked Damascus on July 7 as France’s president is meeting with his Syrian counterpart in a surprise, landmark visit. Emmanuel Macorn was not at the hotel at the time of the explosions and had already arrived in the presidential palace. The two leaders were about to kickoff a series of meetings where contracts regarding Syria's reconstructions were going to be signed when the explosions happened. FRANCE 24's Dana Alboz tells us more from Damascus, and how Syrian authorities are hoping to overcome security challenges to attract investors.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Explosions heard near Macron's Damascus hotel amid high-risk, landmark visit
item.description: Explosions were heard in Damascus as French President Emmanuel Macron is holding a surprise visit to Damascus, hosted by Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa. Macron played a major role in pushing Europe and the United States to drop most sanctions on Syria. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali and Matthew-Mary Caruchet tell us more about the risks and stakes of this visit.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Judgement day for Marine Le Pen
item.description: PRESS REVIEW: Tuesday, July 7 – While Marine Le Pen hopes for an acquittal – or a tad more leniency – over the alleged embezzlement of €4.4 million from the European Parliament, has her party already moved on? In other news, New Zealand and Australia reel after a Chinese missile test in the Pacific Ocean. Finally, the captain of the French national football team responded to the racist tirades of a Paraguayan senator.
item.dc:creator: Antonia KERRIGAN
